title: Postgres as primary datastore
description: All production data lives in a single Postgres instance. Application services connect over a connection pool. No application-level sharding until we cross 100GB or 10k qps, whichever comes first.
rationale: Postgres covers our needs (transactions, JSON, full-text, geo, time-series via extensions) without operational sprawl. We are a small team; one database to operate and back up is the right call until proven otherwise.
trade_offs: Single point of failure until we add a replica
No automatic horizontal scaling — re-evaluating becomes a project, not a knob
Some workloads (high-write event streams) will need additional infrastructure later
alternatives_considered: MongoDB — rejected: we want strong consistency and SQL joins
MySQL — rejected: Postgres extension ecosystem and JSON support are stronger for our use cases
DynamoDB — rejected: vendor lock-in, weak local-dev story, lacks SQL
next_step: Stand up a hot replica before the 90-day check-in
